response: The program is associated with a Bachelor of Science (bsc) degree.
The standard length of the program is six semesters.

Students acquire a total of 180 ECTS credits that are distributed between a required area, a required elective area and an elective area as well as a “Digital Project” module and the Bachelor’s thesis.

The program combines Information processing systems, programs and methods, Economics with a focus on the digital economy, Quantitative methods, mathematics and statistics, and Language, social, communication and decision-making skills (soft skills) and ethics.
Students can choose from the following study profiles: Accounting, Controlling & Taxation, Finance & Economics, Marketing, Organization, Innovation, and Supply Chain Management & Logistics.

The 75 ECTS credits are distributed as 25 ECTS credits in Business Administration and Digital Business, 20 ECTS credits in Information Management, 20 ECTS credits in Quantitative Methods, and 10 ECTS credits in Economics.
The “Digital Project” is explicitly designed for students to learn how to deal with and approach practice-related, digital and/or data-driven problems.
